signup_success_check_email: Success! Check your terminal (dev) or inbox for a confirmation link.

login_required: Please sign up or log in to report an elevator outage.
guide_step_1: 1. Find your building using the search bar or map.
guide_step_2: 2. Tap the big color buttons to tell neighbors if the elevator works.
guide_step_3: 3. If it's broken, use the 'Advocacy Script' to call 311.
guide_step_4: 4. Log your 311 number to track the city's response.
quick_report_title: Is the elevator working right now?
quick_report_help: Tap a button above to tell your neighbors what you see at the elevator. This helps track real-time service for everyone.
log_311_help: If you called 311, enter the number they gave you here. This helps track the city's response.
help_advocate_desc: Provide hard data to elected officials or building management to demand better service.
paper_trail_desc: Track your 311 complaints and legal evidence here.
building_not_found: Building not found. Please double-check the address and try again.
emergency_reports_note: Only use for active emergencies. These reports are flagged as urgent.

verification_explainer: Reports from two different neighbors within 2 hours confirm the status.
no_strategy_available: No advocacy strategy available for this building status.
no_personal_logs: No personal logs. Tap + to add a 311 SR#.

hero_description: Enter your address to view your building's elevator service history, DOB complaints, and tenant reports.
complaint_logged: Complaint logged to your paper trail!
news_sync_started: News refreshes automatically on the 1st and 15th of each month.
status_label_unsafe: Elevator is UNSAFE — Do Not Use
status_label_slow: Elevator is Slow or Having Problems
emergency_help_desc: Use the buttons below to call for help or alert someone who can assist you.
alert_neighbor: Send Text Alert to a Neighbor
alert_neighbor_sms_body: The elevator at this building may not be working. Can you check on your neighbor or call 311?
verification_neighbor_prompt: One neighbor has reported this. Ask another neighbor to also tap a button — once two neighbors agree, the status becomes confirmed.
set_primary_prompt: Search for your building above to start tracking elevator status and set it as your home.
alert_family_sms_body: The elevator is down and I cannot use the stairs. Can you come or call me?
script_label_emergency: What to tell 311 when you call
adv_crisis_heading: New York's elevators are failing its most vulnerable residents.
adv_stat2_sub: Elevator failures at one Bronx development in 2025
adv_stat3_sub: The funding gap NYCHA cannot close alone
adv_impact_heading: A broken elevator isn't an inconvenience. It's a house arrest.
adv_impact_p1: For New York's 100,000 residents in subsidized housing, a broken elevator means being physically cut off from medical care, groceries, and the outside world. Seniors with mobility impairments are left stranded on upper floors — sometimes for days. News12 Bronx reported that when Rosalina Torres's elevator in the Bronx went out, she was unable to leave her sixth-floor apartment for two months — falling multiple times on the stairs while trying to do basic chores.
adv_impact_p2: A 2024 NYC Comptroller audit found that more than 40% of scheduled elevator maintenance tasks go unperformed. And a City Limits investigation found that approximately 25% of buildings transferred to private management have life-threatening health and safety deficiencies — despite being privatized specifically to fund capital repairs.
adv_persona_deidre_quote: When the elevator fails, I can't descend the stairs. I'm trapped for the entire outage.
adv_persona_rosalina_quote: The sign said four weeks. It stayed up for two months while I fell on the stairs trying to get groceries.
adv_persona_martha_quote: I just need to know: can I get out of my building today? My daughter needs to know I'm safe.
adv_solution_heading: Turning neighbor reports into evidence that demands action.
adv_feat1_desc: Two residents. Two hours. Verified. When two different neighbors confirm the same outage within two hours, it becomes a legal-grade record — not just a complaint.
adv_feat2_desc: We calculate the percentage of time a building's elevator is out of service. This number is the language of housing court and council briefings.
adv_feat3_desc: The platform generates a personalized 311 complaint script based on the building's actual outage history. No tenant should have to know the right words — we provide them.
adv_feat4_desc: Every search automatically identifies the building's NYC Council district. One tap connects a resident to their representative's office.
adv_movement_heading: We stand on the shoulders of a century of organizers.
adv_timeline_1904: Immigrant women on the Lower East Side launch NYC's first rent strike, establishing the principle that housing is a public right.
adv_timeline_1920: Mass tenant strikes force New York's first rent control laws — the first time the state regulates landlord profits.
adv_timeline_1970: The warranty of habitability becomes law. Every lease now carries an implied promise of safe, livable conditions.
adv_timeline_2010: JustFix and Fight for NYCHA bring digital tools to tenant advocacy. Data becomes the new organizing weapon.
adv_timeline_now_text: Elevator Advocate gives every tenant the evidence to make every outage count.
adv_builder_bio: This platform is a gift of service to my community — born from watching my neighbors, many of them seniors and wheelchair users, rendered immobile and isolated by failing elevators. Data is how we fight back.
adv_builder_footnote: Open-source · Built during Pursuit AI-Native Cohort 2025
adv_council_heading: Your constituents are stranded. We have the data.
adv_council_body: Every council district in New York has buildings where residents are regularly trapped by failing elevators. We can generate a Loss of Service report for every multi-story building in your district — complete with complaint history, risk scores, and the names of the management companies responsible.
adv_council_footnote: Data sourced from NYC Open Data, official city building records, and resident reports. Updated continuously.
adv_council_cta_primary_aria: Request a district elevator report by email
page_not_found_message: The page you're looking for doesn't exist.
building_not_found_detail: We don't have a record for that building ID. Try searching for your address.
